title: herniaria polymorpha proles maritima
intro: Herniaria polymorpha proles maritima, also known as the sea knitgrass or coastal rudderwort, is a subspecies of the Herniaria polymorpha plant, belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It is a small, annual or biennial plant with white or pink flowers that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Herniaria polymorpha proles maritima include the treatment of kidney and urinary tract issues, as well as its use as a natural remedy for fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
